channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-18099] sanity-pcc test_101a: FAIL: could not map uid 500 to root in namespace
item.description:
This issue was created by maloo for jianyu <yujian@whamcloud.com>

This issue relates to the following test suite run: https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/fc29258e-8e5a-4b28-a13a-9d364a08d28c

test_101a failed with the following error:

== sanity-pcc test 101a: Test auto attach in mount namespace (simulated container) ========================================================== 08:30:05 (1721982605)
CMD: onyx-33vm2 cat /proc/sys/user/max_user_namespaces
CMD: onyx-33vm2 echo 10 > /proc/sys/user/max_user_namespaces
creating user namespace for 500
CMD: onyx-33vm2 runas -u 500 -g 500 unshare -Um sleep 600 &>/dev/null & echo \$!
CMD: onyx-33vm2 ps auxww
Created NS: child (sleep) pid=36544 in 0 seconds
CMD: onyx-33vm2 runas -u 500 -g 500 newuidmap 36544 0 500 1
onyx-33vm2: running as uid/gid/euid/egid 500/500/500/500, groups:
onyx-33vm2:  [newuidmap] [36544] [0] [500] [1]
onyx-33vm2: newuidmap: Could not open proc directory for target 36544
 sanity-pcc test_101a: @@@@@@ FAIL: could not map uid 500 to root in namespace

Test session details:
clients: https://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-master/4558 - 6.8.0-35-generic
servers: https://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-master/4558 - 5.14.0-427.24.1_lustre.el9.x86_64

<<Please provide additional information about the failure here>>

VVVVVVV DO NOT REMOVE LINES BELOW, Added by Maloo for auto-association VVVVVVV
sanity-pcc test_101a - could not map uid 500 to root in namespace
item.summary: sanity-pcc test_101a: FAIL: could not map uid 500 to root in namespace

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.

comments.comment:
"Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>" merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/56312/
Subject: LU-18099 tests: disable userns checking in sanity-pcc/101a
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set:
Commit: 552e7cd3db0aff2e4053c51c6a9a5807adfd4d79

"Feng Lei <flei@whamcloud.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/56312
Subject: LU-18099 tests: disable restriction of userns on ubuntu24.04
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 6b31ec0ef295465ee050fee8adcbd0af277a5212

Feng Lei

Could you please advise on this ticket?

Thanks

Peter

Well, not really the same behavior.
Without '-G 0', you have the error

Cannot change gid to 500/500, errno=1 (Operation not permitted)

and it looks like runas does not even manage to call the command (unshare in this case).

With '-G 0', unshare is called, but it hits an error:

unshare: write failed /proc/self/uid_map: Operation not permitted

I think you should try first without runas, for instance by logging in as uid 500, but a real login, not a su. And call unshare from there. BTW, have you tried the same with other distros, to make sure your test works at least in some environment?

I compiled with the Lustre source code based on your patch:
https://review.whamcloud.com/#/c/fs/lustre-release/+/55474/

The same error:
Test script:

RUNAS_ID=500
RUNAS="/usr/lib/lustre/tests/runas -u 500 -g 500 -G0"

#echo 10 > /proc/sys/user/max_user_namespaces

echo "creating user namespace for $RUNAS_ID"
PID=$($RUNAS unshare -Umr sleep 600 &>/dev/null & echo $!)
ps auxww | grep sleep
echo "Created NS: child (sleep) pid=$PID"
[ -n "$PID" ] || error "remote sleep start failed: $PID"

newuidmap $PID 0 $RUNAS_ID 1 ||
        error "could not map uid $RUNAS_ID to root in namespace"

# with -G0
[sudo] password for qian: 
creating user namespace for 500
running as uid/gid/euid/egid 500/500/500/500, groups: 0
 [unshare] [-Umr] [sleep] [600]
unshare: write failed /proc/self/uid_map: Operation not permitted
root        1064  0.0  0.0   3552  1792 pts/2    S+   08:33   0:00 grep sleep
Created NS: child (sleep) pid=1062
newuidmap: Could not open proc directory for target 1062
could not map uid 500 to root in namespace

# without -G0
creating user namespace for 500
Cannot change gid to 500/500, errno=1 (Operation not permitted)
qian        1085  0.0  0.0   3552  1792 pts/1    S+   08:37   0:00 grep sleep
Created NS: child (sleep) pid=1083
newuidmap: Could not open proc directory for target 1083
could not map uid 500 to root in namespace

Then you should try with runas taken from latest tag from b_es6_0, and specify an explicit supplementary group with '-G' option, just like the patch https://review.whamcloud.com/46493 does.

Alternatively, if you want to stick with master, please apply patch "LU-17961 sec: support supplementary groups from client" at https://review.whamcloud.com/55474 to get the fix for runas.

I compiled and tested with the latest master Lustre branch.
It seems that your patch is not merged into master yet.

It might be a problem with the runas program. See for instance commit fb0082bba1 "EX-4333 sec: support supplementary groups from client" (patch reviewed on https://review.whamcloud.com/46493). I had to modify slightly runas' behavior, and also explicitly specify a supplementary group when calling it in sanity-pcc. Which runas program are you using (i.e. compiled from which Lustre version)?

I did a simple test on Ubuntu 2404, it failed:

RUNAS_ID=500
RUNAS="/usr/lib/lustre/tests/runas -u 500 -g 500"

#echo 10 > /proc/sys/user/max_user_namespaces

echo "creating user namespace for $RUNAS_ID"
PID=$($RUNAS unshare -Umr sleep 600 &>/dev/null & echo $!)
ps auxww | grep sleep
echo "Created NS: child (sleep) pid=$PID"
[ -n "$PID" ] || error "remote sleep start failed: $PID"

newuidmap $PID 0 $RUNAS_ID 1 ||
        error "could not map uid $RUNAS_ID to root in namespace"

failed with

qian@qubt:/root/work/PCC/test$ sh pcc_101a.sh 
pcc_101a.sh: 11: cannot create /proc/sys/user/max_user_namespaces: Permission denied
creating user namespace for 500
Cannot change gid to 500/500, errno=1 (Operation not permitted)
qian       91641  0.0  0.0   3552  1792 pts/1    S+   04:41   0:00 grep sleep
Created NS: child (sleep) pid=91639
newuidmap: Could not open proc directory for target 91639
could not map uid 500 to root in namespace

This is not a bug related to Lustre, it should be something wrong in the command "unshare" or "newuidmap" in the newer kernels.
Yingjin, would you please take a look?

This first started failing on 2024-07-03 and mostly with Ubuntu 24.04 clients (though I found one el9.3 client failure). So it may be that newer kernels are doing something new/strange with namespaces that breaks PCC, or the test is not calling some new command?
